---
title: "Southeast Asia Energy Consumption Visualization Project"
author: "Group 2"
date: "2026-07-13"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default]
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
# Global setup chunk for libraries, options, and shared objects.
```

## Project Overview

This presentation documents a critique and improvement of an existing visualization of Southeast Asia's energy consumption patterns.

## Project Objectives

1. Apply data analysis techniques to generate meaningful insights from structured data.
2. Document data and code to ensure clarity, transparency, and reproducibility.
3. Collaborate effectively as a team to execute a complete, version-controlled data workflow.

## Data Context

- Source dataset: `data/energy_data.csv`
- Scope: Southeast Asia, 2024
- Structure: long-format data with country, fuel type, end-use sector, percentage, and panel fields
- Note: This project uses a structurally faithful synthetic proxy dataset.

### Existing Chart

- [Describe the original visualization here.]
- [List the main strengths and weaknesses here.]

## Visualization Critique

### Key Issues to Address

- [Issue 1]
- [Issue 2]
- [Issue 3]

## Analysis Plan

- [Describe the descriptive analysis to be performed.]
- [Describe any comparisons, summaries, or validation checks.]
- [Describe the insight questions the team wants to answer.]

## Design Direction

- [Describe the improved chart concept.]
- [Describe the intended encodings, layout, and annotations.]
- [Describe accessibility and readability considerations.]

## Reproducible Workflow

- Data cleaning and validation
- Analysis and summary tables
- Visualization generation
- Quarto rendering to HTML

## Deliverables

- `presentation.qmd`
- Supporting data and scripts
- Exported HTML presentation

## Notes

- Fill in detailed findings, figures, and final conclusions later.
- Keep all paths relative to the project root.
